review_text: Great place, grassed drive thru sites, amenities are awesome.. clean as! Dog and kid friendly, great atmosphere.   Quiet but friendly. Close to Blue Lake and Mt Gambier town centre

Brilliant facility with lovely grounds and atmosphere. Fantastic off dog leash area that our two Cavoodles loved. Lovely grassed areas and close enough to everything. At $34. night there was no complaints from us.

Amazing campsite, up there with the very best. Super clean and great facilities, but not too big so still feels cosy. Great pop up events, like wine tasting, gin tasting, waffle van etc. Great location, next to some great natural wonders.

I pulled into Pine Country after my husband had been rushed to hospital for emergency surgery.

I'd never towed a caravan, I had a 4-year-old in tow, and not a single person to help me. Britney and Ken stepped in like family.
Ken patiently talked me through parking the van and got us fully set up and connected. Then both of them checked in throughout the entire week, making sure we were okay and asking how my husband was healing. When you're on the opposite side of the country from your family and feeling completely alone, it's the little things that hold you together. Britney and Ken did that for us.
I can't recommend Pine Country highly enough. Thank you, both of you.

We did the glamping experience, and it was fantastic. The tent was amazing, with everything you need included, and the beds were heated, which made it extra comfortable. There was also a nice fire pit area out the front, with wood supplied.||An ensuite facility is included in this experience, and it was not only modern but extremely clean. The park facilities were great for both kids and campers.||We absolutely loved our time at Pine Country.
We stayed at this Caravan Park with an organised get together with other vanners for 6 nights and we had a lovely time there beautifully maintained park lovely  amenities. Nice spaces.

Absolutly love it here. The best thing is the sites are spacious and well kept. The playground and jumping pillow is great for the kids and the flat land makes it easy for them to ride bikes. The checkin was one of the best i have had, it didnt feel rushed and was very plesent. It is just out side of the main town to feel like you are still camping but can be in the centre of town within a 3min drive.
Good caravan park! Great for kids

Stayed in unpowered site for Easter. One of the nicest caravan parks I have been to. The site and facilities are well maintained.
The lady at the front office was really nice and welcoming.

The unpowered site
- spacious
- you can park your car in your site and still have room
- level grass, not hard ground, easy for tent pegs
- quiet area

Toilets:
- there is a big toilet/shower area near the entrance and a smaller but nicer toilet/shower area (numbered 1-4) near the alpacas. Both are nice and clean but I preferred the privacy of the smaller toilet.

Common kitchen
- well stocked with pots,pans,utensils, plastic plates and bowls
- 2 kettles
- 2 barbecue grills
- 2 sinks (outside and inside)
- one (induction) stove
- microwave

There is also a coffee truck and sitting areas outside the common kitchen.
Alpacas
Dog friendly
Very nice location as well.
Thanks for having us

We stayed here for three nights over the Easter holiday on an unpowered site. The sites are all on grass and quite spacious, and it didn’t feel too crowded even during Easter.

The toilets and bathrooms were very clean. At times there was a bit of a wait, but there is a larger amenities block within walking distance. The kitchen and kids’ play area were also very good.

We would definitely stay here again if we come back to Mount Gambier. The only downside was that on our last morning before check-out, there seemed to be a temporary water and power outage, which left the toilets quite dirty and without running water.
Having had a get together of 17 vans here. We could not have picked a better place. Brittany and Tanya were so accommodating same with the outdoor staff. Spotless facilities and beautiful park

I stayed three nights in this lovely dog friendly caravan park. I had a level, grassy ensuite site which was very convenient; there is an enclosed dog park in the grounds as well. The lawns and gardens were well maintained and the staff was friendly. Mount Gambier is a beautiful place to visit and the Blue Lake is stunning.
Thank you so much for a great stay . Your park has everything and is very clean and well maintained . Would highly recommend this park ..

We loved this park and highly recommend it if you plan to stay in Mount Gambier. The service is perfect and the amenities and grounds are very well maintained. The groundsman is out and about everyday taking care of some part of the yards. Prices were also excellent.

Lovely park ! Great playground and facilities etc however if staying in a cabin take your own hairdryer and iron. Lovely clean cabin just lacked a few things needed especially if a family was staying imo. Not sure why the G’day parks vary in what is provided. We have stayed in others where an iron and ironing board are provided but no big deal at the end of the day!
We stayed in an unpowered site. The park has beautifully kept grounds with good access to sites. The facilities were clean.  We would definitely stay here again.

What a lovely park. Friendly welcoming staff. We stayed for 3 nights. Drive through grassed sites.
It was quiet when we stayed mainly older couples only a few families. They say free range children but I hope that doesn't lead parents to think they can let their kids run feral as so many seem to do. There's a big area for the kids to play so they don't need to be on the roads
The ablutions were clean but they are old and the showers only 4 were cramped. There was plenty of hot water with a good flow. There's a laundry and plenty of clothes lines
Your about 6 to 10 minutes from attractions and shops. I would stay again if back this way and would recommend

What a fantastic park. Green. Trees but not too overgrown to block solar panels and starlink. Quite a range of places to stay and sites.
Friendly staff.
Facilities are older but clean and working. Plenty of toilets and showers. Great camp kitchen and seating area.
Lamas. Just need to add some more animals please💕 maybe chooks?
Little bit out of city but 2 minutes the other side of Blue Lake.
Great park..drivethru bays..staff friendly, helpful and accommodating. Would recommend.
Awsome and clean. Great reading g place for the night.  It is by the road so early morning g traffic 😴

Great park always our first choice always. Great sites friendly staff always there to help or just a quick chat. The showers are looking tired, and need renovation, but the water pressure is fantabulous "don't change it" ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ thank you 😁

We had a powered site. Access was easy, lovely clean facilities, automatic opening gate on exit without a code is nice convenient touch. ON THE WEEKENDS they have independant food truck and coffee van, food and coffee was amazing and added to the experience. Thanks So Much for this fantastic Caravan Park

Great little Caravan Park , Close to Mt Gambier and the Blue Lake , Well run , Unfortunately we arrived on the Friday of a long weekend , Saturday the place was packed and the amenities did struggle , I know it was not management fault but parents who let their kids run a muk , The site next to us had 3 vehicles at one stage , Nice park though ,

The camping ground itself is clean and well laid out and close to the Blue Lake and central Mt Gambier.|There was quite a lot of road noise at night particularly from the semi trailers and the odd 'hoon'. Unfortunately we had one neighbour with a crying baby and another neighbour that smiled and swore a lot which wasn't great. Not the fault of the caravan park though.
My sister in-law and husband are coming to your park referred from me.
We throughly enjoyed our stay in our caravan. Good size sites, good facilities and great staff.
Clean and quiet area. Camp kitchen decently equipped. Wifi slow. Would stop again

This is one of the best van parks we have stayed at. Very friendly lady at reception, and the groundsman did such a great job beautifully maintaining the park and was a really good bloke. The amenities are spotless, loads of green space to walk around, nice level sites, plenty of bins and other general facilities. The off lead dog exercise yard is great. Absolute pleasure to stay there, and we will be back. Thank you.

This park was immaculate. The staff were always friendly and constantly working to keep the place clean and neat. The park is well laid out and very peaceful. Playground and jumping pillow to keep the kids busy. We loved our stay here. One of our favourite caravan parks across Australia so far!

My partner and I stayed in the upgraded 2-bedroom Dog Cabin with our greyhound and had a wonderful experience. The cabin was neat, very clean, and comfortable. The secure outdoor area was perfect for our dog to enjoy some space safely. The design is excellent — we loved our stay and would highly recommend it to anyone traveling with pets.

Tanya at the front office is a superstar! We called to change our booking to arrive the next day (1 day early) and she was super helpful and friendly. Great facilities including showers and camp kitchen with hotplates, microwave, bbq, sinks, cookware, ect. 10/10 recommend
Wonderfully well maintained Park, great location and friendly staff

An excellent caravan park. Have stayed here twice, once on an unpowered site and once on a powered site. One of the few caravan parks that just gets the simple things right. Brittany in the office is extremely helpful and friendly, and the owner Emma is welcoming and accomodating. Amenities are clean, laundry facilities are good, the grounds are incredibly well looked after. Great location, right near the blue lake and prices are very competitive. Good playground for the kids as well as jumping pillow, and more open space than you could ever need. Will definitely stay here each time I’m going through Mt Gambier.

An excellent family-friendly park! We love this place. You get outstanding value for money: it's incredibly clean, well-maintained, and everything is perfectly in order. The staff are lovely and helpful, and our children had a fantastic time. A top choice for a hassle-free, enjoyable stay.

Nice location and the road past is 60km/hr so quiet. The shower and toilet facilities are dated but clean, could do with an upgrade and refresh. Pet friendly with an enclosed paddock for supervised off lead exercise for dogs. Excellent location for trips to port macdonnell and into mt gambier.

Fantastic location very very clean. Park with laundromat there to do washing beautiful green grass to sit on. Fantastic for dogs. There’s a great dog run down the bottom fully fence lovely and soft on their feet. Beautiful park alpacas to go on pat if you got kids really big sites to drive through fantastic park will always go back again.

Stayed in one of the family sized glamping tents for two nights. Was a great experience. Very comfortable parents bed and 15 and 12 yo kids liked their camp beds. Tent was nicely spaced from others, so felt fairly private. Private bathroom was nice to have. Fire pit and firewood in front of tent was great for campfire experience and toasting marshmallows. Also a small gas BBQ area nearby, and could also use park camp kitchen. 5-10 min drive from central Mount Gambier driving past Blue Lake. Would highly recommend as a caravan park stay a bit out of the ordinary.

We had a super lovely stay at Pine Country Caravan Park in Mount Gambier, excellent location so close to the town. It was quiet, peaceful, and very relaxing — exactly what we were looking for. The self check-in process was easy and hassle-free, which made arriving simple and convenient. The amenities were very good, clean, and well maintained, and the park overall felt tidy and welcoming. We really enjoyed our stay and would happily recommend it to others looking for a quiet and comfortable stop in Mount Gambier.
Nice place to go camping. Good service and very quiet at night. A bit pricey, though, for the en suite campground.

Great place to stay for a few days in Mount Gambier! We stayed on a powered site for three nights and had a wonderful experience. The park is safe, friendly, and offers great entertainment for kids.

I especially want to acknowledge the fantastic staff. My son lost his mobile phone during our stay, and it was found and posted back to us days after we had already left. That level of care and honesty is rare and very much appreciated.

Highly recommend this caravan park — it’s one of those places that reminds you why good old-fashioned customer service still matters.

Had unpowered site was great. Shower were always clean. Kids had a ball. Plenty for them to do. Plus we had our dogs they had a leash  free area that they could run around. Camp kitchen was very handy.

Great location |We had a pet friendly cabin...which was fantastic as it was fenced in (perfect). Great there was a fenced in dog exercise park (apart from other guests not picking up after their dogs 😱)|Only negative would be its position right by the entrance (a little noisy at night)|Cabin perfect for our needs...although I would configure differently (use a square folder down table to create more room  and then be able to fit an L shaped sofa....2 seater sofa not adequate for a family of 4)|Would of been nice to be able to change our towels through our stay (one towel each for 7 days, one shower mat, no face cloths....but I didn't ask reception so maybe it might of been possible to refresh these).|Would definitely return though
Beautiful park, easy parking, lovely facilities

We stayed in the dog friendly powered site over Christmas. Site was a good size. Dog area was large and secure for our border collies. Park was very quiet and clean. This is our number 1 preferred place to stay in Mount Gambier

We have been here a few times, but will sadly not make a return trip next time. The cabins condition are poor and an urgent upgrade is needed. The heater is not really working, the hot water runs out at peak times and there is intermittent next to no wifi. Cramped and poor conditions.

Pig sty,toilets stink,booked for three nights only stayed two and got out of the dump,had a clown here with a generator running,would have to be the worst park I've been in,if your after a nice quiet place give this a wide berth,not impressed in the slightest definitely look elsewhere.See attached photo thats what the dog thinks of the place.

This was my first domestic trip in Australia, and I spent a lot of time deciding where to go. I ended up choosing this place after falling in love with the beautiful glamping tent photos — and it was definitely the right choice!

The facilities were extremely clean, everyone was so friendly, and I had such a wonderful and unforgettable experience.

I’ll definitely be back after my next semester finishes! Thank you for making my trip special ✨
Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays! 🎄✨

Camping Review:
Staying on a powered site, we placed a 10 man tent, very spacious, out Navara also parked along side.

I explained at the time of booking that we needed one closer to the toilets if possible due to mobility issues.

Very happy, we were located within a 2 minute walk.

The park is lovely, there is a fenced grassy area for dogs to roam off their lead , that's very spacious.

The park is very well presented and the staff extremely polite.

We chose this caravan park, to support the smaller business and it the best decision this far.

Definitely worth a stay, they obviously have cabins and ensuite caravan sites available also, so if your looking to get away for a couple of nights and what a quiet park. Give them a call, it's within 5 mins drive from the Blue lake. 💙
Very clean and leafy. Good size sites. Staff are lovely.

Stayed in a motorhome for 2 weeks. Well maintained park. Dedicated groundsman. Great  clean amenities. Very short drive to all the attractions .Drive through sites made parking easy for all vehicles. Peaceful and relaxing  Thankyou Pine Country CP
A short 1 night stay in Glamping Tent 4, with Alpacas for company. All needs catered for.
Great service, location and amenities

After staying at over 100 different sites around Australia this trip, we were delighted to come across this gem of a caravan park with a farmstay feel.   Super friendly and helpful staff, clean and tidy amenities, beautiful lush green manicured grass    We stayed down the back in the unpowered and really enjoyed the “farm” feel of the sites.   Highly recommend for all

Pine Country Caravan Park is a great place to stay in Mount Gambier. The location is quiet and peaceful, and the rooms are always clean and well maintained. The park is tidy, the atmosphere is relaxed, and the price is very reasonable. This is where I stay whenever I visit the area, which is very often, and it has always been consistently good. Highly recommend.

Immediate action required: serious neglect of the alpacas

The alpacas at this park are being neglected. Their drinking water is thick with green algae and clearly hasn’t been cleaned or refilled for a long time. This is not a minor oversight, it’s clear negligence and a serious health risk for the animals.

I have emailed the management previously to give them the opportunity to fix this, but the water has not been replaced and I have received no response. The lack of action is alarming.

It is also very upsetting that when it rains, the two alpacas have no shelter at all and are kept in a very small enclosure. Watching them stand in the rain with no protection is distressing and far below any acceptable standard of care.

Immediate action is required. Providing clean water and adequate shelter is the absolute minimum these animals need, and it cannot be delayed any longer.

We came down for our grand daughter's high school graduation and this was our first stay at your park.
We had a drive through powered site.
We found the park more than met our needs.
We had family from Portland visit, with the 2 boys enjoying the jumping pillow and spacious area to kick their football. We loved the spacious drive through site. The only frustration was the very poor mobile phone coverage. I'm aware that you have wifi which we didn't access as the code wasn't on the park map .. and I was too lazy to go back to the office to ask. Hence suggest that you add this to the park map info.
We have been visitors to Mt Gambier for many years as past residents of the town but also we have immediate family living both here and at Portland. We have previously always stayed at the big 4 but fell out with them over the newish visitor policy, which only allows one visitor per paying guest and then at a $15 cost per visitor. Therefore it was such a refreshing delight that visitors were ok at your park.
I would like to add that we found the staff both on the phone and in person to be very friendly and accommodating. This is in starke contrast to the staff at the big 4 who we have found to be the opposite.
We found your facilities to be of a very good standard, but the kitchen area whilst adequate could do with a  indoor sitting area, especially given the weather conditions that we experienced.
The only small down side was the sound of trucks during the week day, but that too was very bearable.
We will be back in only a few short weeks, again for a family gathering, but imagine that we will now be regular visitors to your park.

The caravan sites are drive through, plenty of room for annex and your car. The site was very clean and mowed, and the toilets were very clean. On the weekend they had a food van in for breakfast and dinner. Great spot, we will be back.
One of the best caravan parks I've stayed in with beautiful lawn, lots of shade, clean amenities and friendly staff

Stayed at a powered site and the sites were big, we just had to turned our motorhome around for better grass accessibility and that worked well. The toilets and showers were clean. The shower cubicle was a good size unlike other parks which are pokey. I was showering at dusk and the lights blew so I had to rush. Following morning the issue was rectified. Also, accessible to the blue lake.

Great if you are travelling with dogs or children. Playground for the kids, very large gated area for dogs to run around and play in.

Nice, clean caravan park.

Great park 3 klms out of Mt Gambier. Great relaxing country feel.  Drive through sites options for caravans are spacious and grassed. Fenced off lead dog area is a great idea.  Some cabins are enclosed so that your pooch can enjoy outside time safely.  Spacious green area for kids to run and play.  |The staff are awesomely helpful and happy.|Great family business.  We highly recommend this for a base to explore whilst in Mt Gambier
Great park and priced as it should sites are drive on drive off and green as canbe recommend you stay here

Awesome staff. Great location
Amenities clean and great showers.

Beautiful view and spacious play area for children. Nice kitchen ,clean amenities. Unfortunately we found our cabin not as we expected to be. Dusty and dirty floor, no mop in the cabin so I can't clean it, have to go to Coles bought some paper towel to wiped down the floors. we arrived bit late so couldn't tell the staff in the office as they closed at 5.30pm.  My son found his blanket stained with blood🤔, good thing there is extra blanket that he can use. The electric stove is not really usefull, as I have to wait to cooked like half an hour just to make scramble eggs.
Microwave in some of the cabins is taking up too much valuable bench space. Otherwise perfect and neat as a pin in a tiny house kind of way! Really impressed and would recommend.

Beautiful. Caravan park a wonderful place to stay and relax just take a vacation and break highly recommend staying here for a  holiday the rooms where kept lovely service was great. Very kind staff .we had a great time here beautiful caravan park

We had a great stay at Pine Country Caravan Park! The place was super clean, well kept, and the kids had an awesome time running around.

The food and coffee trucks were a highlight, everything we tried was delicious. The only downside was the wait time.

Our bungalow was small but had everything we needed. We’d planned to enjoy the fire pit we ordered, but the rain spoiled that part.

Overall, a really nice spot for a family getaway. We’d definitely recommend it!

Beautiful scenery, great amenities for an affordable price. We were squashed between two campervans with kids so it was not as pleasant, BUT it's still a beautiful caravan park to wander about and sleep at regardless

This park is one of the worst that we have unfortunately stayed at.
Amenities block old and very ordinary.
Camp kitchen small and open to the elements. So close to the centre of town and yet no phone service. Wifi was provided for 500mb , that lasted 5 minutes. We had paid for 2 nights but left after the first.
Plenty of parks in the area and I would recommend going elsewhere.

Great place to stay nice park friendly service.
Will stay again
A nice quiet park on the outskirts of Mt Gambier, very neat and tidy and friendly staff.  I would highly recommend this park.
Great off lead area for dogs and it even has fenced dog cabins and a fenced car van site.  The gardens and facilities are well maintained.  I will be back

We stayed for 5 nights at a powered site. Was close to amenities & the grounds were beautiful to walk around. Staff are lovely & very helpful. We even hired a fire pit for a night, which was very enjoyable.m
Friendly people, super-helpful. Clean, modern facilities.
Nice calm relaxing time. Lots to do around the city. 👍
Great experience glamping!

Nice, quiet spot surrounded by trees and plenty of space between sites. The cabins were clean and comfy, and the park felt well looked after. Staff were friendly and helpful, made check-in easy.

It’s not far from the main town and attractions like the Blue Lake. Good place to stay if you’re doing a road trip or just need a few nights to relax.

🏕️Quiet and pretty camping site.

I booked online and the website already gave me a good first impression. Clean design and easy booking process.

When I arrived a lovely lady helped me out. My booking was already prepaired so it was a quick and easy check in.

The park itself is very well looked after, large but cute. The location is 1km from main Mount Gambier so it’s nice and quiet during the nights.

They offer various types of accommodation and all in all looked very good.

There are multiple toilets spread out throughout the park and all the toilets and showers are clean.

I liked their camp kitchen area, they have cute lighting and have all the amenities you need for cooking.

If you are travelling with children then this is also a good spot as they have swings, playhouse and jumping area. + Alpacas 🦙

There was staff taking care of the park and you can feel that they make sure everything is up to standard.

The price was very fair. I paid 30$ per night for an unpowered site.

Overall great stay and would definitely come again.
Beautiful little park . Lovely helpful girl in office . Everything was so clean and well cared for thank you guys  . Would def stay again .

Greta location just outside of the town.
Ammenities were clean and plentiful, nice hot showers and laundry facilities. Can’t comment on rooms as we camped in a powered site.
Option to add on a fire pit hire and wood was nice.
You are close to other campers but it’s a caravan park so not sure what else you would expect. Wasn’t an issue for us as we were here in winter so a fair bit quieter.
Lovely little town and site is close to the blue lake.
Friendly staff and easy check in. Would absolutely stay again.

If you are passing through absolutely check out the sink hole garden in town!!
Great park, very clean and well kept amenities and kids area. Easy to navigate sites, friendly service. We ended up extending our stay as we enjoyed the area so much. We will be back, thanks guys!
Amazing camping site … they have all facilities…. Well maintained area

Don't often write reviews but was really happy with our stay here.  Staff made us most welcome, the dog friendly cabin - with attached little yard - was great and the location was convenient and quiet.  Thanks!
Very good caravan park highly recommended

Great park close to town and quiet.
Exelent camp kitchen and facilities.

We had previously had to cancel our  two night Camp site due to unforeseen circumstances. We had a credit to use and due to the weather change decided to use the credit on a cabin instead of towing  our van  from Victoria and bought friends along on the trip:.  So they hired a cabin also. The cabins  we stayed in are out dated, noisy, and  drafty  , Electric blanket faulty  in one cabin the heating was inadequate.     Bed linen clean but to thin, for cold night . Both parties were agreeable to leave  and find other accomodation.  Was an expensive trip   Can not recommend to anyone  .
Great, quiet Park. Powered and unpowered sites, cabins and these great tents. We only stay here when in Mt. Gambier
Great caravan park. Dog friendly and an off lead area as well. Close to everything.
Such a beautiful peaceful park. We stayed in the clamping tents and the set up is so considerate and homely, they really do a fantastic and thoughtful job as hosts.
Lovely caravan park. Close to attractions.

Lovely caravan park, with excellent facilities which are very clean. Great location for a base to explore Mt Gambier & surrounding areas. Has big sites, cabins & glamping tents. Rates per night were great, considering most caravan parks are now very overpriced. Really enjoyed our stay here & would totally recommend.
A very nicely maintained caravan park and not expensive either!
Friendly staff,big sites clean and tidy ,some traffic noise.

Nice caravan park overall. Clean and easy access inside the park. Amazing facilities as well including playground for kids and laundry.

Cabin is quite old but still okay to stay. It was clean as well.

Location is great, not so far from the Blue Lake. 🙂

My 1st trip to Mount Gambier. Solo lady traveller with a vintage Pop top Van and dog. Powered site was lovely and easy to manoeuvre in and out. Fabulous park, great amenities, Fabulous service and very welcoming. Couldn’t be happier. My dog Lolly and I will be back on October.  Thank you for an enjoyable stay!
Best campsite in MG. Family run business, it shows they care.

We stayed in the 1970s made Cabins. A little bit dated but acceptable. Wooden cupboards have seen better days. It does the job though, fridge is ok, air con is ok, electric blanket was provided which was a nice touch. Despite other cabins being seperate, you can hear your neighbours stomping about. The park is otherwise quiet and not far from attractions.
Well recommended place to book and stay pet friendly with all amenities
Average sites very tight

Had a wonderful stay here
Easy check in the site was big just a bit dusty but we had a mat
The code to enter the park is fantastic more parks should copy this 👍🏻

I was forced to hire the last available room, which was a glamping tent. Due to other accomodation falling through. The amenities in the tent are as pictured and are okay, but the mini fridge is impossibly small, does not make ice, a microwave with no microwavable plates. It was summer, 40 degrees outside and the tent several degrees hotter, this is really dangerous. The staff never opened the ventilation windoes, even though they knew it was booked, increasing the heat issue when we arrived at 12pm. I was told at booking that it had not been done and i had to do it myself. My makeup even melted in the tent indicating the levek of heat. It was like being in a hot car. It was completely unusable until after 7pm due to the heat. A small fan was provided but this is not sufficient. We had to spend the day in the car with the aircon. For the price, a portable aircon is required for safety. There is no shade around the tent until the sun begins to set. The WiFi does not reach you at all, the other campers are awfully close, and the only 2 close toilets are inbuilt with the showers. This was for at least 100 people nearby. People also guarded the toilets and waited for spouses to leave the other toilet, so they could then shower together. MOST having no regard for the time they are in there. They need separate toilets for people as it was painful and uncomfortable waiting to use the toilet and did not even have a chance to shower. I suffered heat stroke and nearly ended up in hospital. It would be much nicer in any season than summer, the animals are sweet to look at... but I would not return, ever, at any time of year.
Just outside town, but brilliant location

Fantastic stay for our family in the cabins however I was disappointed to learn on check in that the jumping pillow had been out of order for 3 months. My kids were looking forward to this, and I don’t think it should still be advertised if it’s not available. Other than that, a lovely stay.

We stayed at Pine country CP for 1 night
The location is just a few mins away from the city.
This CP is clean, quiet and well equipped
The campers here are friendly and the staff members were polite and courteous
We were very happy and fortunate to have stayed here on site 101
What a great place to stay, tried an ensuit site for the first time, a good idea

Camped one night (Fri) at an unpowered site. All sites were very well maintained. Nice lawn, not dusty. Nice and quiet. Facilities were on the older side but functional. Showers (tiles) could do with a good scrub. If you're looking for a cheaper park without the hoards of people, this is the place.
Beautiful park, easy to find and very tidy. Worth a stop for sure.
Great park and location however I didn’t read that it was pet friendly and had to listen to barking dogs throughout the night

What a very beautiful caravan park
Thick green grasses drive through site with little shade trees to put your chair under as was very hot when I stayed February 2025 abd pet friendly as I had my cat & little dog

The owner or manager is such a polite lovely lady

The park also has a huge very well maintained thick grasses paddock to allow your dog to run off leash

2km short walk to mt gambier blue pool the large water catchment with shading views on a platform with all locks people donated on the fencing

Beautiful town with everything you need

Such a beautiful caravan park shd place to stay
Great park, friendly staff and short drive to everything. Very clean amenities. Stayed 4 nights here in van, a great place to visit and a great CP & a good price …thanks Pine Country CP 🤙👍
Powered site excellent drive through, amenities, very clean and tidy..

great park! Close to Blue Lake and the other attractions of Mount Gambier.
So much open space, Alpacas to look at, clean amenities.
we had powered site 1, it has a fenced area for dogs! fantastic!
and there is a large fenced off-leash area within the park.
love it!

Had an unsuited site, well prepped and clean.
The park is well laid out & signed.

Grosser Platz. Die Lage war leider nicht ideal, zu Fuss kommt man leider nirgends hin. Der Platz ist sonst aber gut und sauber. Die WC- Anlagen sind eher alt aber waren sehr sauber, von daher total ok. Wir waren nur eine Nacht da und dafür war es gut.

Only 4 showers for a large caravan park. Had to queue each night. Nothing  to dry hands on. Quite dirty. Campers too close too each other with very noisy neighbours so late nights and early mornings with most waking around 5am! Would expect a lot better for the price we paid
Great location,lovely and quiet no noise from the road and great facilities
It was average
Grand camping avec peu de douche, WC, sanitaires.  Il faut choisir son heure, sinon c'est pénible.  Il y a de la musique dedans ce qui est très agréable.

I recently stayed at Pine Country with friends and was extremely disappointed. The customer service was unacceptable, and the manager's behavior came across as overtly racist, which was deeply unsettling. The facilities were poorly maintained and far below the standard I’ve experienced at other camping grounds in South Australia. Unfortunately, this was by far the worst camping experience I’ve had."

Recently stayed there for one night as we were passing through the town. They greatfully welcomed our last minute booking for an unpowered site. Great facilities and showers as well as camp kitchen for those who need it. Would have happily extended our stay if we had time!

We had an amazing new year at this place. We stayed in their dog friendly cabins and it was great. Our doggy daughter loved being in the backyard. The cabin was in very good condition with everything we needed to make our stay unique. The location is perfect, 10-15 minutes from the best of Mt Gambier. A place to come back 10/10.

Thanks Pine family.
Gen, Clau and Gala.

Fabulous park, drive through sites are large and lush. Amenities are clean, great old school playground / equipment. First caravan park we’ve been to with a dog park, the BEST idea ever. We (us and the dogs) thoroughly used and loved the dog park!

I recently stayed at Pine Country in one of their two pet-friendly cabins, and I couldn’t be more impressed! The cabin was incredibly spacious, spotless, and well-equipped with all the necessary cooking utensils, making it feel like a true home away from home. The beds had electric blankets, which was a lovely touch for extra warmth and coziness.

The air-conditioning in the cabin was excellent, keeping the space heat and comfortable throughout our stay. Another highlight was the backyard, which was fully gated, providing a safe and secure area for our dogs to enjoy.

The amenities throughout the park were also very clean and well-maintained, adding to the overall positive experience. I highly recommend Pine Country Caravan Park for anyone looking for a peaceful, comfortable, and pet-friendly getaway!
Never go there again, rude staff, trucks route very noisy morning time,

We stayed one night but the people and place was clean and very well kept.

Defintley would stay again!

We stayed in a studio cabin for a few nights in Dec2024. Clean room with all necessities including cooking gear. Bathroom was a short stroll away and wasn’t busy. Internet was bad. During peak hours mobile data was slow. Wifi passcode provided was no use as we were too far away from the office.

Absolutely disgusting customer service and extremely rude staff! We were given a campsite right next to the toilets, and when I politely asked to move to one of the many empty sites I could see, the staff rudely dismissed me, saying they were “allocated to someone else.” They wouldn’t even explain why or try to help. It felt like they couldn’t care less about our experience. I don’t understand how everyone pays the same, yet we’re stuck with the worst spot without any flexibility. This made no sense since we didn’t pay any less than anyone else, and there’s no option to lock in a site when booking. Why isn’t it first-come, first-serve if everyone pays the same? The attitude of the staff was appalling—no respect, no courtesy, just plain rude. Worst campsite I’ve ever been to. Avoid this place at all costs!

We stayed overnight in one of the Pine Country Caravan Park's un-powered camping sites. We booked with them because they were the best deal ($$$-wise) we could find for our overnight stay in Mt. Gambier. We were so glad we made this choice. The park is immaculate and in a very beautiful & peaceful setting - and the communal shower/toilet facilities were immaculate. I would highly recommend any campers, caravaners, cabin-dwellers or glampers (their glamping sites looked amazing!!) to make this park their first choice when staying in Mt. Gambier. Thank you so much Emma and Nathan!
Unpowered sites have lush views, quiet and clean. Love the music / radio playing in the bathrooms

Great park and facilities.
Our kids loved the jumping pillow and the staff were super friendly and engaging with our kids and their travel passports.
Thanks for a great stay :)

A very nice Caravan park. Very peaceful and beautiful. I
Booked a Pet Friendly Dog Cabin first impression was terrible. It was so small. Hot as ever the air conditioning didn't work properly. Did not look how the pictures were on the website at all. Only stayed 5 hours and left as it was not pleasant. All the owners need to do is up grade the dog cabins and it would be great place to stay.
I got my refund back but just a terrible experience.
😪😪😪

Great park. Very clean.
Most sites are drive through with lush grass. Amenities were good. Lovely hot showers.
My dog loved the off leash park. Would definitely recommend to anyone. I've stayed at other parks in Mt Gambier and this is by far the best.

Amazingly beautiful camp ground... drive through sites plus other grasses sites powered and uncovered. Very busy park but quiet. Great paddock for the dogs, and alpacas on sites as well.. wake up to the mooing of cows... the flamping tents look stunning. Very affordable accommodation. Dog friendly, great amenities and minuted from Blue Lake which is also stunning. Easy check in and friendly staff.. highly recommend 👌
Great green place, great site, great facilities. Close to town
An amazing spot to stop for a few nights. An added bonus was the off leash dog area that we really enjoyed. Loved the camp kitchen area! Thanks so much!

My wife and I stayed overnight in a dog friendly cabin with our two greyhounds. We're very happy with our choice - the grounds are immaculate, room very clean and equipped with a comfortable bed and reverse cycle AC. Kitchen and bathroom are a little dated but clean and functional.

The park has a big off leash area for the dogs to run in and the small fenced yard of the cabin is convenient for quick pre-bed or first thing toilet breaks for the dogs too.

We would happily stay again!

Stayed overnight, never again due to the road directly outside the park which is a bypass route for trucks which need to by-pass the Blue-Lake reserve area due to the narrow 2 lane road leading over the Blue Lake mountain reserve into Mt-Gambier.
Had booked a cabin which was old & in need of a renovation. The bathroom was a experience in itself, it so reminded me of grandma's old bathroom of the 70's.  the cabin was not only old, but cold too.  Only given location 3 stars due to the closeness of the lake.

This is a great caravan park just outside Mount Gambier.mease of access to town is wonderful.
The abultion blocks were clean and well maintained.
Camp kitchen superb. Staff friendly and helpful.
Would come back in a heart beat.
Do try the scrolls from shop up the road,  such s different experience.
Interior of 2 bedroom cabin is very dated. very cramp, only enough space to walk.  happy with the bed.  Price is rather high. Not value for money.

One of the best caravan parks we’ve stayed in. Beautiful location looking out at acres of green farm land with cows and alpacas.
Clean amenities and a really nice feel to this caravan park. Highly recommend.

Really lovely this caravan park. I’ve travelled a great deal of Australia and this place is right up there. Very fair prices. With members discount we paid around $35 a night powered. The amenities and facilities provided are great. The staff are lovely. The grounds are great and located next to a farm it’s a picturesque spot to enjoy a cold one watching the cows and alpacas do their thing. Definitely consider these guys over the more expensive BIG4 down the road. I would 100 percent go back here.

Great place in gorgeous surrounds.
Really tranquil. Breathtaking.

Great park and staff friendly and helpful. Drive through sites are great and excellent having no codes for amenities and boom gate on auto for exits. Easy drive to town and there is plenty to see with the lakes and sinkholes. 100% recommend.

Nice facilities, pretty location.

Camp kitchen is well loaded with appliances, although hot water (in the kitchen) was not working when I was there.

Very affordable rates, suited my needs.

A fabulous caravan park. Staff are super helpful and friendly. Facilities are spotless.
Plenty of site options. Walking distance to Scroll Queen bakery
I didn't use the cabins but a unpowered camp site, toilets constantly cleaned, and the staff friendly and helpful recommend staying if you are in Mt Gambier SA
Lovely caravan park with clean amenities and pleasant staff. Great location to base yourself to explore this beautiful area and city.
Great park friendly hosts great facilities

Very impressed with this park!
The drive-through bays offer ease of entry and stacks of room.
As a couple who travel with our dog, the off-leash area is highly prized and our Groodle loved it!
Amenities are kept spotless and they've got everything you need.
Will definitely be back the next time we travel to Mount Gambier!

My wife and I stayed 3 nights at Pine Country Caravan Park on a drive through site. From the moment we arrived we were looked after very well by the staff.  The ablutions were spotless and well maintained. The park grounds were well maintained. We had a lovely stay in Mount Gambier and this caravan park and I highly recommend for fellow caravaners to come and visit this caravan park. Thankyou very much for your hospitality it's appreciated.
Well managed beautiful park.
passed through for a night would love to have stayed longer. very clean amenities, friendly staff. the park was simple but great for the kids.
Made welcome, great location for local activities, yet quiet.very well set up camp ,looked awesome if you have kids
Long weekend in Mt Gambier, an awesome  quiet caravan park on the fringes on Mt Gambier
Very clean
Beautiful family owned park. Perfect new roads throughout the park. Clean and tidy amenities. The staff were very friendly and helpful throughout our stay. We have rebooked for next holidays.
Very clean and nice place, amazing owner that offered us a late check out for free
The staff are great...it's on a truck bypass so you'll get traffic noise ...the facilities are kept very clean.
Stayed in a non powered camp site. Site was nice and green. Beautiful view looking over hills. Lovely staff, very friendly. Stayed two nights. Park very clean, I would highly recommend.

Large drive through powered sites. With plenty of room to set up our tandem van and park the car. The park is a 5 min (or so) drive out of town and there are a few small shops about 400m up the road toward the lake.

Great place to stay, dog friendly with a very large off leash dog area fully fenced, fantastic amenities, very clean, well kept park,  Big drive through site both powered and unpowered, adult only or family glamping areas and cabins which from the outside look well maintained.  Friendly, helpful staff which always helps.

We absolutely loved our stay here! For our first time visiting Mt Gambier, the lovely people at Pine Country Caravan Park ensured that I had a reserved site next to my friends who had checked in days earlier. Super easy check in and access whenever we needed. Everything was spotless, the bathrooms and toilets were the cleanest I've ever been to... They even had music playing in them too - what a luxury and for a bargain! Next time we're visiting, we'll be back for sure.
Nice clean dog friendly

We have just had a lovely 3 night stay over the weekend here on a ensuite site, this park was amazing could not fault a thing. Lovely large drive thru sites, clean amenities very well kept grounds and very friendly staff. Can highly recommended this park expecially if travelling with pets, nice leash free area for a run.
Blaze loved his time exploring mt gambier. Will definitely make this our first point of call next time in mt gambier
Thanks for a lovely stay.
Very quite location, very clean and tidy. Our little family enjoy staying at the park. Go back anytime.
Dog friendly, close to blue lake, good bed, fantastic shower,  cheaper than stayz.

Our last night at this park tonight - and it has been a delight. We're on an unpowered site, with plenty of room for an 18ft caravan (no neighbours whilst here - the staff do a good job of placing people away from each other). Big tick to the well maintained facilities and lovely grassed sites (the dog says thank you). Open spaces aplenty, including paddocks (and content looking cows) down two sides of the property. Nice and relaxing country vibes. Clearly a well managed park.

Staff were always friendly and helpful, even when our (quite large) group managed to overload the circuits in the camp kitchen at 8pm due to our overly ambitious cooking. Thank you very much for the stay.
Clean , very nice place lovely people. Been there one night on our Road trip we loved it !
Quite busy but vquietly

Friendly staff (Brittany was lovely and helpful), great amenities, and very dog friendly. The only downside is the size of the blocks, which are uncomfortably narrow. We had people on either side with large caravans and it was not enjoyable feeling like you are in one another’s personal space.
Good location, nice area, but for us, we wouldn’t book again because of the lack of privacy and space.
Stayed at a powered site. Easy check in. Very neat park. All facilities kept tidy. Appreciate the rounds the staff do keeping everything in check. Thanks
We had our own caravan. Drive through sites✔️, Very quiet compared to the other park located on the same road but on the roundabout. Great playground and bouncy pillow for kids.
Having a few drinks and people complain. Poor form. Manager came around an said keep it down. Poor form. Won't be stopping Here again
Nice caravan Park. I was with a small van in an unpowered site and site were good size and flat. Facilities were cleaned.

We had the most wonderful stay at this park. Amenities are outstanding and clean.
Thanks so much for helping me to nurture the seed for future tent adventures with my family, we all loved it!!
First caravan park we’ve stayed at in SA as we often travel Victoria. It did not disappoint. Clean facilities, well maintained park and friendly staff.

Beautiful grounds, clean and family friendly!
Pine Country isn't the largest park at the Mount but it would be our return visit.
All facilities well maintained, plenty for the kids to do and additional holiday visits from Wineries, Distillers and food vans while we were there.
The staff were very friendly and always available to help, even understanding our kid's frequent B choices in building their own nature play hut from fruit trees 🤦🏻‍♂️
While we didn't bring a pet, it's perfectly setup for it.
Highly recommended ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Easy access. Easy check-in. Helpful Staff. Thoughtful facilities.
This is one of the best and cleanest camp park I've been to. Very close to everything and if you need your latte made in the morning, there's a coffee truck scheduled to come. Wonderful place.
Thank you for hosting us and for the amazing service! We booked a powered spot for a night. Amenities were very clean (plus nice hot showers) and price was very reasonable.

Great location to visit the sink holes and caves in the area. Family friendly Caravan park and they even came to give us lolly bags for the kids on Christmas Day. Facilities were always clean. Would be very happy to stay there again.

Decent caravan park to stay in peak periods. Showers and toilets were clean, just distant from the unpowered area.

Great location. Quiet at night.

We stayed here for one night on a whim while passing through to beachport, lovely park and such clean amenities. Definitely wish we had of stayed longer, one of the best places we’ve stayed in and very reasonably priced!
Cabins are outdated paper thin walls and about 40 degrees inside on a 28 degree day. Air con barely kept up. Time for an upgrade imo
Schöner sauberer Park. Waren eine Nacht mit dem Camper dort. Die Ausstattung ist sehr gut, das Personal sehr freundlich.

All the deposit money was taken and the rest of the money was instantly taken away even of we had left early we'd have to pay the whole thing for the designated date. The unpowered sites cost the same as the powered sites which I don't think one tent and a caravan costs over $300 (powered sote). This cost us the exact same for the unpowered site. We had enough room for at least one more tent but we're told we'd had to pay another $50! Just for a three man tent. Apart from this the quality was great and the location was perfect.

Pine Country Caravan Park would have to be one of the tidiest, greenest, cleanest, kid friendly caravan parks we’ve been to. The staff were so lovely and accommodating, our kids made friends with the kids who live/own the park as well as others.
The off leash dog park was situated over the septic tanks (no smell of course) which meant nice grass in a huge area for the dogs to run around, benches under shade for humans. 10/10 would recommend!

I booked an impromptu night in one of the Glamping tents in November and was impressed and comfortable enough that I requested to stay an additional night.

Being more used to Caravans and swags, I wasn't sure what to expect but the bed was comfortable, the private bathroom was new and clean and the tent was decked out with everything I needed to have a nice few nights away despite the average weather. The Glamping area (and the park in general) was also quiet and tidy and somewhere that was relaxing spending time in.

Overall, my Glamping experience at Pine Country was more enjoyable than the hotel room and the caravan park cabin I stayed in at other locations during the same trip for a similar price - I'd definitely go back and also recommend the park to friends and family.
A really nice place to stay, I had an unpowered site and all the facilities were excellent- everything I needed was there 😁
Beautiful and gorgeous place so we really enjoyed our time and stayed
Neat and tidy cabin with a nice little yard for our pooch to run around in. Great location. Within easy driving distance of the lakes
Wonderful park. We went in the winter. Sites were grassy. The dog park was also a great touch.

Great park friendly  staff,  but if you need to use wifi think again  about this park , when we Rang to book was not told about very poor wifi and when we checked in again was not informed  about poor  wifi  had to go back to office and ask for a wifi code , again that was a waste off time  very poor wifi  walk out on the main road you may get service. I do understand  this is not the parks fault  but it would be nice to let customers  know.

Der Camping und die Sanitäranlagen sind sehr sauber. Idealer Standort für den Blue lake und die Sinkhole zu besuchen.
Wir wurden freundlich empfangen

Really nice park. Very friendly staff and felt very welcome
Lots of space in the unpowered sites and beautiful grass.
The only small let down was the tiny showers, I could barely get dressed. However super hot water and best water pressure.
By far the nicest and cleanest caravan park we experienced on our trip from Cairns to Adelaide. Modern, clean, beautiful, perfect! Wish all parks were this good! ☺️
Glamping was great! Lovely view. Have a private bathroom a short walk from tents
Very popular lucky to get a site
Very nice park, staff are friendly, price is very reasonable. We recommend this park.
Very large powered grass sites, clean facilities
Stayed 1 night, loved the location (just outside of town). Good sized sites, clean grounds and amenities. Loved the offering of a fire pit and wine package. Would definitely return.

We absolutely loved our 5 nights camping at Pine Country Caravan Park. The facilities are some of the best we experienced in our three month trip; a generous camp kitchen (including air fryer!), bouncing pillow and playground and the best shower pressure! The park was neat and spacious and we loved being able to hire a fire pit (with wood, kindling and newspaper) to keep warm at night. Thanks for the great stay!
Very nice clean and affordable park dog friendly all sites grassed

What a great park , had a Ensuite powered site , great shower, great service , park entry code easy, no annoying text messages during our stay , booking , payment and access on arrival seamless, we will be back
Beautiful park. Louise at front desk very lovely. Highly recommend this caravan park. Would definitely stay here again.
Absolutely Beautiful Place we'll be back
The park and amenities were clean and well maintained. Dog friendly. We enjoyed our stay,, looking forward to our next stay here
We really enjoy our stay and feel very relaxing.
Very Friendly.  Dog Friendly.   Clean amenities

This has to be one of the prettiest park we have stayed in drive thru sites manicured grassed areas and a huge off leash dog area. If your thinking of caravanning to Mount Gambier I highly recommend this caravan park only a couple of mi utes out of Mount Gambiers main street.

We stayed here for 2 nights in an RV. I highly recommend staying here if you are staying in Mt Gambier and we will definitely be back. The staff were very friendly and very helpful. A very well kept property, quiet location and the sites and amenities were also excellent especially the toilet/shower block. The music playing in the toilet/shower block was a nice touch.
We glamped in the tents and the kids loved it. Cant wait to come back :)
Friendly staff
Clean rooms, friendly staff and oh so comfortable bed in the cabin. Wifi slow and the 4g signal patchy, not a deal breaker.
